stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho nears Chelsea exit as Aston Villa plot loan move for unwanted star
stories.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a season-long loan with a conditional obligation to buy next summer. The 22-year-old winger, signed by Chelsea from Manchester United for £40m last year, has struggled with just 12 goal contributions in 43 appearances. Villa have agreed terms, completed a medical, and are finalising paperwork. The deal follows Morgan Rogers’ £117m move to Chelsea in the opposite direction.

stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
stories.summary: Aston Villa has secured a deal to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea on a season-long loan, with a conditional obligation to buy next summer. The agreement meets Chelsea's valuation of £43 million, following Garnacho's underwhelming stint at Stamford Bridge, where he contributed just 12 goals in 43 appearances. The 22-year-old Argentine has already completed his medical and is set to finalize the paperwork in Birmingham. This move marks a fresh start for Garnacho as he joins Villa under manager Unai Emery.

stories.title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old French modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, near Paris, on Monday. Siad was under investigation for allegedly aiding Epstein in recruiting and trafficking young women for sexual abuse, claims he denied. An autopsy has been ordered to determine the cause of death, and authorities have opened an inquiry into the circumstances surrounding it. His death adds to the ongoing scrutiny of Epstein's network and the pursuit of justice for survivors.

stories.title: Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 MK Dons: Top 10 talking points
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur began their preseason campaign with a win in a behind-closed-doors friendly against League One side MK Dons at the Tottenham Hotspur training ground. With several players missing due to the recently completed World Cup, a mix of youth and experience was on display as Roberto De Zerbi heavily rotated his side each half. New signings Mateus Fernandes, Sandro Tonali, and Martin Dubravka made their debuts, and it was the former who was the difference, with his stunning strike the lone goal in a scrappy encounter. Here’s my top ten talking points from Spurs’ first outing under Thomas Frank. 1. Where’s Waldo?

stories.title: Rosalía breaks her silence after sparking outrage among Argentine fans: “My bad, sorry."
stories.summary: Rosalía, after sparking outrage among Argentine fans for a social media post supporting Spain during the World Cup, issued a brief apology and deleted the offending tweet. She explained that she had shared without reading the caption, leading to misunderstanding. The incident highlights the potential consequences of miscommunication in public statements.

stories.title: Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons: Mateus Fernandes marks debut with screamer as Spurs open pre-season with a win
stories.summary: Tottenham opened their pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons at Hotspur Way on their debut outing under Roberto De Zerbi. New signing Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal in the third minute, curling a first-time volley off the post. Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka also made their Spurs bows in the behind-closed-doors friendly. De Zerbi’s side will face more pre-season tests as they aim to gel new arrivals ahead of the new campaign.

stories.title: Romano: Summerville accepts Al Hilal’s offer
stories.summary: Crysencio Summerville has accepted a lucrative offer from Saudi Pro League club Al Hilal, leaving AS Roma empty-handed after a last-minute bid. The transfer, valued at £65 million including add-ons, sees the 24-year-old Dutch winger heading to Saudi Arabia for a medical examination. Roma will now shift their focus to other potential signings as they seek to strengthen their squad. This move underscores Al Hilal's aggressive recruitment strategy in the current transfer window.

stories.title: 'Nobody ever spoke about her': Australian woman traces family link to Tuam mother and baby home
stories.summary: Allara Kate Ashton believes an 11-month-old boy who died at the Tuam mother and baby home in 1925 was her grandaunt’s child, as she seeks DNA evidence to confirm a long-buried family story. This discovery comes amid ongoing investigations at the former St Mary's home in County Galway, where 99 sets of infant remains have been uncovered since searches began last July. The search, led by the Office of the Director of Authorised Intervention Tuam, has collected a total of 62 DNA reference samples to aid in identification efforts.

stories.title: Après l'interruption du concert de Barbara Butch, Aurore Bergé accuse ouvertement LFI d’antisémitisme
stories.summary: French DJ Barbara Butch was forced to leave a concert in Grenoble on July 18 after a violent protest by pro-Palestine and La France Insoumise (LFI) activists. The incident has sparked accusations of antisemitism, with LFI's Mathilde Panot acknowledging a "bad" tract but denying any wrongdoing. The controversy has led to a public defense of Butch by Minister Aurore Bergé, who has also met with the DJ to discuss her safety and protection. Butch is scheduled to perform again on July 25 in Saint-Martin-du-Tertre, raising concerns about her security.

stories.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
stories.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met as children through family friends, arrived at the hospital in Madrid after Lucía went into labor. The name honors Belén Rodríguez, a close friend of Pombo’s who passed away in April. The newborn joins the family’s growing next generation, following the birth of Pombo’s cousin earlier this year.

stories.title: El Supremo admite el recurso para parar las altas en el censo por la Ley de Nietos y pide audiencia a la JEC
stories.summary: The Spanish Supreme Court has admitted a petition by Iustitia Europa to suspend new voter registrations under the controversial "Law of Grandchildren," which allows descendants of Spaniards to vote from abroad. The court has given the Central Electoral Board ten days to respond on suspending the measure, which has already granted voting rights to over 300,000 people. The ruling follows multiple complaints alleging electoral manipulation. A final decision is expected as legal challenges intensify ahead of key elections.

stories.title: Arthur (Secret Story) découvre la moitié du secret de Simon, son intitulé exact est désormais connu
stories.summary: Arthur a découvert la moitié du secret de Simon dans Secret Story mercredi 22 juillet, identifiant l’intitulé partiel « Je suis champion de natation ». Simon a confirmé la partie liée à la natation mais a précisé que le secret complet reste inconnu. La révélation a déclenché un buzz massif sur les réseaux sociaux, alimentant les spéculations. Les prochaines confrontations pourraient lever le voile sur le reste du mystère.

stories.title: « Je suis de retour » : pour la quatrième fois, Kylian Mbappé va figurer sur la pochette d'EA Sports FC
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappé returns as the face of EA Sports FC 27, marking his fourth appearance on the cover after a four-year absence. The game launches September 25, 2026, with Mbappé now representing Real Madrid on the cover. He joins Lionel Messi as the only players to appear four times on the franchise’s cover. The announcement follows his record-breaking World Cup goal tally.

stories.title: Peter Lim visita Valencia para ver en persona a Inma Ibáñez y supervisar el progreso del Nou Mestalla
stories.summary: Peter Lim, dueño del Valencia CF, visitó Valencia tras el Mundial 2026 para supervisar las obras del Nou Mestalla. Acompañado por directivos, recorrió el estadio en nueva fase constructiva, con 50 pilares de acero instalados para la cubierta. También se reunió con Inma Ibáñez, directora de la Fundació VCF. La visita marca su regreso a la ciudad tras siete años.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 results: Luke Humphries suffers shock first-round loss to Cameron Menzies
stories.summary: World number two Luke Humphries suffered a shock first-round exit at the World Matchplay Darts on Monday, losing 10-7 to Scotland’s Cameron Menzies. The 27th-ranked Menzies stunned Humphries with a maximum 170 checkout, becoming the third unseeded player to advance. Humphries, who had won the US Darts Masters in June, will now regroup after a slow start to the year. Menzies faces 15th seed Ross Smith in the next round after Smith’s 10-6 win over Kevin Doets.

stories.title: Svolta nella lite tra tennisti e tornei: ecco il passo storico del Roland Garros
stories.summary: Roland Garros will become the first Grand Slam to share event revenue directly with players, breaking a long-standing dispute over prize money. The French Open’s move follows threats from top athletes, including world No. 1 Jannik Sinner, to withhold media commitments unless their share rose from 15% to 22%. Details remain undisclosed, but the shift to a revenue-sharing model signals a potential industry-wide change. Other Slams now face pressure to follow suit before their next editions.

stories.title: Furto di 80 fiale di Fentanyl all'Israelitico, due custodi indagati a Roma
stories.summary: Two custodians at Rome’s Israelitico Hospital are under investigation for stealing 80 vials of fentanyl, a powerful opioid. The theft was discovered in late June 2026, with initial shortages reported on June 23 and a full inventory revealing the loss by June 24. Authorities suspect the drug may have been diverted to an illegal medical network rather than street drug markets. Investigators are now tracing the stolen fentanyl’s final destination.

stories.title: "¿Quieres que se le recorte la pensión?" La sindicalista Afra Blanco estalla contra una empresaria que pide bajar las cotizaciones
stories.summary: A heated TV debate erupted over pension cuts after a businesswoman proposed reducing social security contributions to boost take-home pay. Afra Blanco, a union leader, accused her of targeting future pensions, while the businesswoman cited a 52.5% take-home pay from a €5,780 employer cost. The clash highlighted opposing views on Spain’s welfare system during a cost-of-living crisis. Next, lawmakers may review tax relief for low-income earners.

stories.title: Verdict of accidental death at Kerry teenager's inquest
stories.summary: A jury has returned a verdict of accidental death for 14-year-old Thomas Healy, who died when the car he was driving crashed into a tree on Ross Road, Killarney, on July 7, 2021. The car was being pursued by a Garda van, and the crash occurred after the driver reportedly panicked. His 15-year-old passenger survived with severe injuries. A Garda investigation in 2022 cleared the pursuing officer of any wrongdoing.

stories.title: FIFA sends message to Nigeria ahead of 2030 World Cup qualifiers
stories.summary: Nigeria's chances of qualifying for the 2030 FIFA World Cup depend on stability in the country's football structure, according to FIFA, which acknowledges Nigeria's rich pool of talent but notes that success will hinge on a robust structure for reliable performances. The Super Eagles failed to qualify for the 2026 World Cup, finishing second behind South Africa in Group C of the African qualifiers. Nigeria has a history of impressive footballing production, but stability is key to achieving success. The country's football administrators will need to address this issue to improve the team's chances.

stories.title: Frankfurt: 70-Jährige soll auf Ex-Freundin ihres Sohnes eingestochen haben
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman is in custody in Frankfurt, accused of stabbing her son’s ex-girlfriend with a knife near the Eiserner Steg on Sunday afternoon. The victim, 37, suffered superficial cuts to her neck and face. Both the woman and her 47-year-old son face charges of attempted murder and aggravated assault. Two bystanders intervened and held the suspect until police arrived.

stories.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra2 und die Galaxy Watch9: Smarte Begleiter am Handgelenk
stories.summary: Samsung unveils the Galaxy Watch Ultra2 and Galaxy Watch9, featuring AI-driven wellness insights and improved battery life. The Ultra2’s 800 mAh battery boosts runtime while reducing thickness to 10.9mm. Both models retain the classic design with a circular display and three side buttons. Wear OS 7 with OneUI 9.0 powers the Ultra2, offering deeper fitness tracking.

stories.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
stories.summary: Harry Potter keert voor het laatst terug naar de Nederlandse bioscopen met een speciale speelweek van 26 augustus tot 2 september. Ter gelegenheid van het 25-jarig jubileum van de eerste film organiseert Pathé een 24-uurs marathon in de nacht van 29 op 30 augustus. De kaartverkoop start op 31 juli, de verjaardag van Harry Potter. Bezoekers kunnen kiezen uit volledige of halve marathons in elf geselecteerde bioscopen.

stories.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
stories.summary: Major League Soccer resumed Wednesday, July 22, with a full 15-match slate after a World Cup break. Inter Miami hosted Chicago Fire in a game that could feature Robert Lewandowski’s MLS debut. Lionel Messi’s availability remained uncertain ahead of the match. The league continues with another 15 games Saturday before the July 29 All-Star Game against Liga MX.

stories.title: “È stata la notte più terrificante della mia vita”: Alberto Rossi perde il suo barboncino Ares, ore di angoscia e poi la svolta
stories.summary: Italian actor Alberto Rossi's dog Ares, a brown barboncino, was found after being missing for hours in Caprera. Rossi had been searching for the dog since it escaped during a bike ride, prompting a desperate appeal on social media. Ares was eventually located thanks to the efforts of friends and a drone, and Rossi has expressed gratitude to the community for their support. Rossi and Ares are now back home, where they will undergo a check for ticks.

stories.title: France pesticide fight : Macron rejects Monique Barbut’s resignation as farm law opens disputed exemptions
stories.summary: France’s environment minister Monique Barbut tried to resign July 22, 2026 over a new farm law allowing controversial pesticides, but President Macron rejected her departure. Parliament had just passed the emergency agricultural law the day before, reopening use of acetamiprid and flupyradifurone despite her objections. Barbut called the exemptions a step backward and said she lacked political means to continue. She remains in post as the government stands by the law.

stories.title: Stevenage vs West Ham LIVE Score Updates in Friendly Match
stories.summary: West Ham United beat Stevenage 3-1 in a closed-doors preseason friendly at Lamex Stadium on July 22, 2025. Over 50,000 season ticket holders watched the match free on West Ham TV after the club waived the £5 streaming fee. Nuno Espírito Santo’s side, fresh from a 1-1 draw with Southend, now turn focus to their 2025-26 campaign. The fixture sold out entirely to season ticket holders.

stories.title: French Model Scout Daniel Siad Linked To Epstein Found Dead
stories.summary: French authorities opened an investigation after Daniel Siad, a modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, west of Paris on Wednesday. Prosecutors said Siad was under investigation in France for allegedly helping Epstein recruit and traffic young women, charges he denied. His name appears in over 1,000 records tied to Epstein’s network from declassified documents. Siad also faced a rape allegation from a former Swedish model.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Flight Dynamics Research Facility in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. Agency Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will join the ribbon-cutting ceremony. The event is open only to U.S. media citizens or permanent residents, who must RSVP by July 29. The facility marks a key upgrade for NASA’s aeronautics research capabilities.

stories.title: In our opinion: Toyota’s Koji Sato’s Japan commonization strategy is sound, difficult — and familiar
stories.summary: Toyota CEO Koji Sato is advocating for parts standardization among Japan's automakers to enhance competitiveness against China's cost advantages. This strategy, reminiscent of Sergio Marchionne's earlier attempts, faces challenges due to U.S. antitrust laws and a history of price-fixing among suppliers. If successful, this initiative could reshape collaboration in the industry and strengthen Japan's automotive sector.

stories.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
stories.summary: Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett testified Tuesday before a House oversight task force examining diversity, equity and inclusion policies in law schools. She alleged systemic racism persists across institutions, calling it pervasive in every setting. The hearing focused on DEI programs in legal education amid broader debates over their impact. A report on findings is expected within weeks.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Fifteen people were injured, including one seriously, in a tram collision on the Erasmus Bridge in Rotterdam on Wednesday morning. The accident occurred around 11:10 AM when one tram collided with another, causing significant damage and debris on the street. Emergency services responded quickly, treating some victims on-site while others were transported to the hospital. The bridge is currently closed to all traffic as investigations continue into the cause of the crash.

stories.title: MATCH PREVIEW | VALENCIA CF – CD ELDENSE
stories.summary: Valencia CF face CD Eldense in a pre-season friendly at the Antonio Puchades Stadium on July 25 at 8:00 PM. This marks Valencia’s first match at the venue following training in Girona. Eldense, promoted to LALIGA HYPERMOTION, last met Valencia in the 2024–25 Copa del Rey, where Valencia won. Free tickets for VCF members are available from July 20 to 23.

stories.title: Philipsen wins stage 17 and reopens a battle for the green jersey that seemed closed
stories.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026, a 174.7 kilometer race between Chambéry and Voiron. The Belgian rider from Alpecin-Premier Tech took the victory, with Mauro Schmid second and Olav Kooij third. Philipsen's win reopens the battle for the green jersey, closing in on leader Mads Pedersen. The general classification remains unchanged, with the favorites now facing a decisive alpine triptych.

stories.title: Schumacher zwengelt Verstappen-geruchten opnieuw aan: "Daar zijn gesprekken gevoerd"
stories.summary: Red Bull driver Max Verstappen's future remains uncertain as his manager Raymond Vermeulen and team principal Jos Verstappen were spotted at the McLaren hospitality during the Belgian GP weekend. Ralf Schumacher has reignited speculation over Verstappen's future, citing the unlikely coincidence of their presence. Verstappen has yet to comment on his plans, but most experts believe he will remain with Red Bull in 2027. The Dutch driver's future will likely be clarified in the coming months.

stories.title: 'It was easy, really': Inside Awa Fam's speedy transition from Spanish champ to Seattle Storm rookie sensation
stories.summary: Awa Fam made history on April 13, 2026, becoming the highest-drafted Spanish player ever selected No. 3 overall by the Seattle Storm. Instead of immediately joining the team, she spent a month balancing Valencia Basket’s Liga Feminina playoffs with remote WNBA training via Zoom calls. Fam helped Valencia secure a fourth straight league title before making her WNBA debut on May 24, playing 20 minutes off the bench in her first game. Seattle coach Sonia Raman called her transition a crash course, throwing her straight into the fire.

stories.title: Dream School maakt doorstart met nieuwe gezichten
stories.summary: Dream School krijgt een doorstart met een nieuwe schoolleiding. Voetbalscheidsrechter Danny Makkelie wordt de rector en Ouassima Tajmout de mentor, vervangend Eric van ’t Zelfde en Lucia Rijker. Het achtste seizoen verschijnt in 2027 op televisie. De omroep hoopt op een frisse start met de nieuwe presentatoren.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: The Committee on Authorisation to Proceed has rejected the request by prosecutors at the Rome Public Prosecutor’s Office to obtain the chat records between Andrea Delmastro and the restaurateur Mauro Caroccia , who is in custody on mafia charges. Meanwhile, MPs from the Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in the Senate in protest, holding up placards reading ‘release the chat logs’. The sitting was suspended. The occupation of the government benches lasted over half an hour. “Shame on you, shame on you,” shouted the Five Star Movement senators. The centre-right responded in unison: “Clowns, clowns”.

stories.title: Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after the indictment
stories.summary: Former Spanish President José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow following his indictment for influence peddling on May 19. The interview comes after his appearance at the National Court on June 17. This marks his first public response since the legal proceedings began. Observers expect further developments as the case progresses.

stories.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
stories.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess potential health factors. Authorities called officers after Fakir was found in a confused state and resisted attempts to calm him. The incident has sparked debate over police use of force and legal protections for officers.

stories.title: JK Rowling offers support to Women’s Rights Network after Féile cancellation
stories.summary: JK Rowling has publicly backed the Women’s Rights Network Northern Ireland after Féile an Phobail cancelled its Respects, Rights and Reality event. The festival organisers said the event’s focus on women’s sex-based rights clashed with its commitment to marginalised groups, including transgender rights. The network called the cancellation shocking and blamed an organised online campaign targeting its members. The group plans to reschedule the discussion.

stories.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, calling them a "signifier of modernity" that disrupts realism. The director made the comment during a Tuesday interview with Gayle King, emphasizing the shoes' potential to break immersion in period productions like his latest film, "The Odyssey." While actors like Matt Damon and Anne Hathaway navigate ancient Greek settings, Nolan insists comfort must not clash with historical authenticity. The rule applies across his productions, ensuring continuity in his meticulous visual storytelling.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: Conservative Party chair Kevin Hollinrake has asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the payment was personal and unconditional, while the Conservatives question if it was compensation for running as an MP. HMRC has not yet commented on the inquiry. Farage has previously stated the funds were for personal security and Brexit campaigning.

stories.title: Monaco close in on €30m deal for Nantes’ Matthis Abline
stories.summary: Monaco are finalising a €30 million deal to sign Nantes striker Matthis Abline. The 23-year-old has agreed a five-year contract, making him Nantes’ record sale after their relegation to Ligue 2. Monaco aim to bolster their attack as Folarin Balogun may leave following his World Cup success. The move is expected to be completed soon.

stories.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
stories.summary: Sophie Thatcher, known for her dark roles in Yellowjackets and Heretic, is shifting gears. The 22-year-old actor, who grew up in a devout Mormon household, is stepping into lighter projects. Next up, she stars in the upcoming thriller The Boogeyman, set for a June 2 release.

stories.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar resigned to join Andy Burnham’s government, accepting a peerage and a ministerial role at the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. Rivals in Holyrood accused him of hypocrisy, citing past criticism of the House of Lords. The SNP demanded he donate his salary to charity. The move follows Sarwar’s earlier pledge to abolish the Lords.

stories.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s new prime minister, faced fresh scrutiny after backtracking on income tax and rent policies within days of taking office. On Wednesday, he confirmed freezing private sector rents remains under review despite earlier signals it would be abandoned. Burnham admitted his off-the-cuff remarks during the Makerfield byelection had been misinterpreted, calling the reaction exaggerated. The government now faces pressure to clarify its economic plans as the opposition demands consistency.

stories.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
stories.summary: Thousands of fans are expected to attend the LVII Trofeo Colombino match at the Nuevo Colombino stadium in Huelva, Spain on Wednesday. Due to high expected attendance, fans are advised to arrive early and consider alternative parking options as most nearby areas will be closed for Fiestas Colombinas setup. A designated parking area will be available near Gol Norte, accessible from Avenida del Decano. Traffic congestion is anticipated, potentially causing delays at the stadium's entrances.

stories.title: FCC Chair Says ABC’s Decision to Not Broadcast Trump Speech Live Will Be Part of Agency’s Review of Station Licenses
stories.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says ABC’s decision to skip live coverage of Donald Trump’s July 16 speech on election security will be reviewed as part of the agency’s early license checks for eight ABC-owned stations. Trump, who claimed China accessed millions of voter files to manipulate past elections, criticized ABC and NBC for not airing his remarks live and suggested their licenses should be revoked. Carr, a Trump appointee, indicated broadcasters may have an obligation to carry presidential addresses. The FCC’s review process could take months.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers will appeal a four-match touchline ban given to manager Derek McInnes for calling a late penalty against Celtic "disgusting." The Scottish FA issued the ban after his comments about a stoppage-time spot-kick in Motherwell on May 13, where Celtic won 3-2. McInnes, who was Hearts manager at the time, claimed the decision was incorrect and will contest the penalty. He is set to miss Rangers' opening Scottish Premiership game.

stories.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes faces a four-match touchline ban for calling Celtic’s late penalty against Motherwell "disgusting." The Scottish FA handed the ban over comments made after Hearts’ 13 May loss, where Celtic won 3-2 to stay level on points before beating Hearts to the title days later. McInnes, who was Hearts manager at the time, insists his criticism was aimed at the decision, not referees. He will miss Rangers’ opening Scottish Premiership fixture.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. French prosecutors confirmed an investigation into his death and noted Siad’s name appeared thousands of times in released Epstein files. Authorities had previously investigated Siad for alleged human trafficking and conspiracy but found insufficient evidence for arrest. The case remains under review despite his death.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals a €1.5 billion allocation for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set to take effect in October. This move is part of a broader €8.5 billion package that includes €7 billion in spending increases, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027. The government aims to keep more of workers' pay through personal taxation changes. The statement's publication by Minister for Finance Simon Harris and Minister for Public Expenditure Jack Chambers marks a key milestone in shaping the country's economic strategy.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league confirmed it is reviewing the move following a settlement between Miami and the LA Galaxy over Casemiro’s discovery rights. MLS has not disclosed the settlement terms and will not comment until the investigation concludes. The league’s rules grant a club first refusal over a player on its discovery list.

stories.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
stories.summary: A robotic servicing mission launched from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, carrying NASA-supported technology to service satellites in geosynchronous orbit. The Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV), equipped with NASA’s Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload, will use advanced robotics to inspect and upgrade spacecraft by installing propulsion modules. Funded by DARPA and developed with the U.S. Naval Research Laboratory, the mission aims to extend the lifespan of existing satellites. The MRV represents the first multi-mission robotic in-space servicer, testing new capabilities for future space operations.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani reversed his pledge to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of legal authority. Mamdani now urges US officials to enforce the International Criminal Court’s arrest warrant, though the US is not an ICC member. Netanyahu faces allegations of war crimes, with Trump vowing he would not be detained during a US visit. Mamdani’s team confirmed no legal avenues exist for local enforcement.

stories.title: Machtwechsel im Osten?: Alle Daten zur Landtagswahl in Sachsen-Anhalt
stories.summary: A major shift in power is expected in the eastern German state of Saxony-Anhalt as voters head to the polls on September 1, with over 1.7 million eligible citizens set to determine the new balance of power in the state parliament. According to recent polls, the Alternative for Germany party could secure up to 41% of the vote, potentially making it the strongest force in the state. This would be a significant change from the current power dynamics. The outcome of the election will be closely watched as it could have implications for the country's political landscape.

stories.title: Israel may equip IDF with anti-submarine aircraft as tensions with Turkey grow
stories.summary: Israel's defense establishment is considering purchasing the Boeing P-8 Poseidon anti-submarine aircraft, a maritime patrol and multirole plane capable of detecting submarines and carrying various weapons. The aircraft, used by several countries, has an operational radius of 2,200 kilometers and can be refueled in flight. The purchase is reportedly in response to growing tensions with Turkey. The IDF currently operates the "Oron" strategic intelligence and surveillance aircraft, but the P-8 Poseidon would provide enhanced capabilities.

stories.title: Aston Martin secures £550m loan deal
stories.summary: Aston Martin secured a £550 million loan to strengthen its finances amid ongoing struggles. The luxury carmaker, headquartered in Gaydon, Warwickshire, reported a 50% jump in net losses to £493.2 million last year and plans to cut 600 jobs. The loans aim to support future product plans and improve its balance sheet. Half-year results are due on July 29.

stories.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
stories.summary: Wolves’ first public pre-season match of the summer takes place at Maidenhead United this evening. The National League South side hosts Cesar Peixoto’s team in Berkshire on Wednesday 22 July, with Wolves TV providing live coverage starting at 7.30pm. Fans can watch the free stream by signing into their Wolves account, though the match won’t be available on the Wolves App. The next pre-season fixtures will be streamed on Wolves TV for £5 each.

stories.title: Il sindaco di Bologna soffia sul fuoco
stories.summary: A violent protest in Bologna turned chaotic, leaving 64 police officers injured and six police vehicles damaged. The unrest occurred during a rally called by the city's mayor, Matteo Lepore, in response to the death of Abderrahim Fakir, a 43-year-old man who died during a police intervention. The incident marks the second major disturbance in Italy this week, following a 25th anniversary commemoration of the 1997 G8 protests in Genova.

stories.title: Assoluti: 1044 a Firenze, Jacobs, Battocletti e...
stories.summary: A record 1044 athletes are set to compete in the Italian National Championships in Florence, Italy, from July 24 to 26. The event marks the city's return as host after 22 years, featuring top athletes including Marcell, Nadia, and Fabbri. The championships will award 40 Italian titles, with the first round, the Challenge, taking place on Friday and Saturday. The competition will conclude on Sunday, setting the stage for Italy's European team selection.

stories.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
stories.summary: Chelsea FC is on the verge of breaching Uefa's financial rules, having spent between £164m and £210m on player transfers since Xabi Alonso became manager. The club faces a £2.6m fine, but can write off £1.7m if they reduce expenditure and increase revenue by next summer. Chelsea's squad value is estimated at £1.3bn, second only to Manchester City in the Premier League. The club's significant debts remain a concern despite recent player sales generating over £120m.

stories.title: Dublin Airport is about to boom. Why is Aer Lingus shrinking itself?
stories.summary: Aer Lingus is reducing its flights and staff by 7.7 percent, despite Dublin Airport's passenger cap being lifted and charges set to decrease. This decision comes as International Airlines Group, Aer Lingus' parent company, focuses on operating margins across its subsidiaries. IAG's investment strategy allocates funds based on operating margins, with airlines achieving 12 to 15 percent margins receiving additional investment. Aer Lingus' operating margin is expected to be closely monitored in the coming months.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
stories.summary: Austrian defender Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. Wöber joined Leeds in 2023 and made 19 appearances in his debut season, including eight league appearances in the 2024/25 title-winning campaign. He scored one goal for the club. Wöber's departure comes after a loan stint with Werder Bremen last season.

stories.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
stories.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: MPs urge Britain to target ultra-processed foods to cut obesity rates. A health committee report highlights sugar and refined carbs as key drivers, not fat or salt alone. Current policies focus on fat reduction, pushing manufacturers to replace it with sweeteners. The call comes as obesity costs the NHS billions annually, with calls for stricter reformulation targets.

stories.title: Las redes sociales de À Punt superan el millón y medio de seguidores
stories.summary: À Punt’s social media presence has surpassed 1.5 million followers across all platforms, marking a 32% average growth in the last year. The chain’s Facebook page leads with over 269,000 followers, followed by Instagram with 228,000. Digital content production has also surged, with TikTok posts up 45% in early 2026.

stories.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians were sentenced to 11 and eight years in prison in Angola’s capital, Luanda, for terrorism and spying. Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov were convicted of inciting protests, spreading disinformation, and interfering in next year’s presidential election. Authorities linked them to a Russian political network tied to the Wagner group. Their lawyers sought extradition to Russia, but the court’s decision remains unclear.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged in the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African tourist Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors say the investigation, opened in May 2025, led to formal charges after initial custody in 2025. Carter, a mother of four who managed holiday rentals, was found near her car with stab wounds. Authorities continue to review evidence as the case proceeds.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials report no new diagnoses in six days, with 56 patients discharged and eight still hospitalized. The outbreak stems from cooling towers on Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. Further test results are pending as the city continues its investigation.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho nears Chelsea exit as Aston Villa plot loan move for unwanted star
offbeat.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a season-long loan with a conditional obligation to buy next summer. The 22-year-old winger, signed by Chelsea from Manchester United for £40m last year, has struggled with just 12 goal contributions in 43 appearances. Villa have agreed terms, completed a medical, and are finalising paperwork. The deal follows Morgan Rogers’ £117m move to Chelsea in the opposite direction.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa has secured a deal to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea on a season-long loan, with a conditional obligation to buy next summer. The agreement meets Chelsea's valuation of £43 million, following Garnacho's underwhelming stint at Stamford Bridge, where he contributed just 12 goals in 43 appearances. The 22-year-old Argentine has already completed his medical and is set to finalize the paperwork in Birmingham. This move marks a fresh start for Garnacho as he joins Villa under manager Unai Emery.

offbeat.title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
offbeat.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old French modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, near Paris, on Monday. Siad was under investigation for allegedly aiding Epstein in recruiting and trafficking young women for sexual abuse, claims he denied. An autopsy has been ordered to determine the cause of death, and authorities have opened an inquiry into the circumstances surrounding it. His death adds to the ongoing scrutiny of Epstein's network and the pursuit of justice for survivors.

offbeat.title: Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 MK Dons: Top 10 talking points
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur began their preseason campaign with a win in a behind-closed-doors friendly against League One side MK Dons at the Tottenham Hotspur training ground. With several players missing due to the recently completed World Cup, a mix of youth and experience was on display as Roberto De Zerbi heavily rotated his side each half. New signings Mateus Fernandes, Sandro Tonali, and Martin Dubravka made their debuts, and it was the former who was the difference, with his stunning strike the lone goal in a scrappy encounter. Here’s my top ten talking points from Spurs’ first outing under Thomas Frank. 1. Where’s Waldo?

offbeat.title: Rosalía breaks her silence after sparking outrage among Argentine fans: “My bad, sorry."
offbeat.summary: Rosalía, after sparking outrage among Argentine fans for a social media post supporting Spain during the World Cup, issued a brief apology and deleted the offending tweet. She explained that she had shared without reading the caption, leading to misunderstanding. The incident highlights the potential consequences of miscommunication in public statements.

offbeat.title: Tottenham 1-0 MK Dons: Mateus Fernandes marks debut with screamer as Spurs open pre-season with a win
offbeat.summary: Tottenham opened their pre-season with a 1-0 win over MK Dons at Hotspur Way on their debut outing under Roberto De Zerbi. New signing Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal in the third minute, curling a first-time volley off the post. Sandro Tonali and Martin Dubravka also made their Spurs bows in the behind-closed-doors friendly. De Zerbi’s side will face more pre-season tests as they aim to gel new arrivals ahead of the new campaign.

offbeat.title: Romano: Summerville accepts Al Hilal’s offer
offbeat.summary: Crysencio Summerville has accepted a lucrative offer from Saudi Pro League club Al Hilal, leaving AS Roma empty-handed after a last-minute bid. The transfer, valued at £65 million including add-ons, sees the 24-year-old Dutch winger heading to Saudi Arabia for a medical examination. Roma will now shift their focus to other potential signings as they seek to strengthen their squad. This move underscores Al Hilal's aggressive recruitment strategy in the current transfer window.

offbeat.title: 'Nobody ever spoke about her': Australian woman traces family link to Tuam mother and baby home
offbeat.summary: Allara Kate Ashton believes an 11-month-old boy who died at the Tuam mother and baby home in 1925 was her grandaunt’s child, as she seeks DNA evidence to confirm a long-buried family story. This discovery comes amid ongoing investigations at the former St Mary's home in County Galway, where 99 sets of infant remains have been uncovered since searches began last July. The search, led by the Office of the Director of Authorised Intervention Tuam, has collected a total of 62 DNA reference samples to aid in identification efforts.

offbeat.title: Après l'interruption du concert de Barbara Butch, Aurore Bergé accuse ouvertement LFI d’antisémitisme
offbeat.summary: French DJ Barbara Butch was forced to leave a concert in Grenoble on July 18 after a violent protest by pro-Palestine and La France Insoumise (LFI) activists. The incident has sparked accusations of antisemitism, with LFI's Mathilde Panot acknowledging a "bad" tract but denying any wrongdoing. The controversy has led to a public defense of Butch by Minister Aurore Bergé, who has also met with the DJ to discuss her safety and protection. Butch is scheduled to perform again on July 25 in Saint-Martin-du-Tertre, raising concerns about her security.

offbeat.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
offbeat.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, on July 21. The couple, who met as children through family friends, arrived at the hospital in Madrid after Lucía went into labor. The name honors Belén Rodríguez, a close friend of Pombo’s who passed away in April. The newborn joins the family’s growing next generation, following the birth of Pombo’s cousin earlier this year.

offbeat.title: El Supremo admite el recurso para parar las altas en el censo por la Ley de Nietos y pide audiencia a la JEC
offbeat.summary: The Spanish Supreme Court has admitted a petition by Iustitia Europa to suspend new voter registrations under the controversial "Law of Grandchildren," which allows descendants of Spaniards to vote from abroad. The court has given the Central Electoral Board ten days to respond on suspending the measure, which has already granted voting rights to over 300,000 people. The ruling follows multiple complaints alleging electoral manipulation. A final decision is expected as legal challenges intensify ahead of key elections.

offbeat.title: Arthur (Secret Story) découvre la moitié du secret de Simon, son intitulé exact est désormais connu
offbeat.summary: Arthur a découvert la moitié du secret de Simon dans Secret Story mercredi 22 juillet, identifiant l’intitulé partiel « Je suis champion de natation ». Simon a confirmé la partie liée à la natation mais a précisé que le secret complet reste inconnu. La révélation a déclenché un buzz massif sur les réseaux sociaux, alimentant les spéculations. Les prochaines confrontations pourraient lever le voile sur le reste du mystère.

offbeat.title: « Je suis de retour » : pour la quatrième fois, Kylian Mbappé va figurer sur la pochette d'EA Sports FC
offbeat.summary: Kylian Mbappé returns as the face of EA Sports FC 27, marking his fourth appearance on the cover after a four-year absence. The game launches September 25, 2026, with Mbappé now representing Real Madrid on the cover. He joins Lionel Messi as the only players to appear four times on the franchise’s cover. The announcement follows his record-breaking World Cup goal tally.

offbeat.title: Peter Lim visita Valencia para ver en persona a Inma Ibáñez y supervisar el progreso del Nou Mestalla
offbeat.summary: Peter Lim, dueño del Valencia CF, visitó Valencia tras el Mundial 2026 para supervisar las obras del Nou Mestalla. Acompañado por directivos, recorrió el estadio en nueva fase constructiva, con 50 pilares de acero instalados para la cubierta. También se reunió con Inma Ibáñez, directora de la Fundació VCF. La visita marca su regreso a la ciudad tras siete años.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 results: Luke Humphries suffers shock first-round loss to Cameron Menzies
offbeat.summary: World number two Luke Humphries suffered a shock first-round exit at the World Matchplay Darts on Monday, losing 10-7 to Scotland’s Cameron Menzies. The 27th-ranked Menzies stunned Humphries with a maximum 170 checkout, becoming the third unseeded player to advance. Humphries, who had won the US Darts Masters in June, will now regroup after a slow start to the year. Menzies faces 15th seed Ross Smith in the next round after Smith’s 10-6 win over Kevin Doets.

offbeat.title: Svolta nella lite tra tennisti e tornei: ecco il passo storico del Roland Garros
offbeat.summary: Roland Garros will become the first Grand Slam to share event revenue directly with players, breaking a long-standing dispute over prize money. The French Open’s move follows threats from top athletes, including world No. 1 Jannik Sinner, to withhold media commitments unless their share rose from 15% to 22%. Details remain undisclosed, but the shift to a revenue-sharing model signals a potential industry-wide change. Other Slams now face pressure to follow suit before their next editions.

offbeat.title: Furto di 80 fiale di Fentanyl all'Israelitico, due custodi indagati a Roma
offbeat.summary: Two custodians at Rome’s Israelitico Hospital are under investigation for stealing 80 vials of fentanyl, a powerful opioid. The theft was discovered in late June 2026, with initial shortages reported on June 23 and a full inventory revealing the loss by June 24. Authorities suspect the drug may have been diverted to an illegal medical network rather than street drug markets. Investigators are now tracing the stolen fentanyl’s final destination.

offbeat.title: "¿Quieres que se le recorte la pensión?" La sindicalista Afra Blanco estalla contra una empresaria que pide bajar las cotizaciones
offbeat.summary: A heated TV debate erupted over pension cuts after a businesswoman proposed reducing social security contributions to boost take-home pay. Afra Blanco, a union leader, accused her of targeting future pensions, while the businesswoman cited a 52.5% take-home pay from a €5,780 employer cost. The clash highlighted opposing views on Spain’s welfare system during a cost-of-living crisis. Next, lawmakers may review tax relief for low-income earners.

offbeat.title: Verdict of accidental death at Kerry teenager's inquest
offbeat.summary: A jury has returned a verdict of accidental death for 14-year-old Thomas Healy, who died when the car he was driving crashed into a tree on Ross Road, Killarney, on July 7, 2021. The car was being pursued by a Garda van, and the crash occurred after the driver reportedly panicked. His 15-year-old passenger survived with severe injuries. A Garda investigation in 2022 cleared the pursuing officer of any wrongdoing.

offbeat.title: FIFA sends message to Nigeria ahead of 2030 World Cup qualifiers
offbeat.summary: Nigeria's chances of qualifying for the 2030 FIFA World Cup depend on stability in the country's football structure, according to FIFA, which acknowledges Nigeria's rich pool of talent but notes that success will hinge on a robust structure for reliable performances. The Super Eagles failed to qualify for the 2026 World Cup, finishing second behind South Africa in Group C of the African qualifiers. Nigeria has a history of impressive footballing production, but stability is key to achieving success. The country's football administrators will need to address this issue to improve the team's chances.

offbeat.title: Frankfurt: 70-Jährige soll auf Ex-Freundin ihres Sohnes eingestochen haben
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman is in custody in Frankfurt, accused of stabbing her son’s ex-girlfriend with a knife near the Eiserner Steg on Sunday afternoon. The victim, 37, suffered superficial cuts to her neck and face. Both the woman and her 47-year-old son face charges of attempted murder and aggravated assault. Two bystanders intervened and held the suspect until police arrived.

offbeat.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra2 und die Galaxy Watch9: Smarte Begleiter am Handgelenk
offbeat.summary: Samsung unveils the Galaxy Watch Ultra2 and Galaxy Watch9, featuring AI-driven wellness insights and improved battery life. The Ultra2’s 800 mAh battery boosts runtime while reducing thickness to 10.9mm. Both models retain the classic design with a circular display and three side buttons. Wear OS 7 with OneUI 9.0 powers the Ultra2, offering deeper fitness tracking.

offbeat.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
offbeat.summary: Harry Potter keert voor het laatst terug naar de Nederlandse bioscopen met een speciale speelweek van 26 augustus tot 2 september. Ter gelegenheid van het 25-jarig jubileum van de eerste film organiseert Pathé een 24-uurs marathon in de nacht van 29 op 30 augustus. De kaartverkoop start op 31 juli, de verjaardag van Harry Potter. Bezoekers kunnen kiezen uit volledige of halve marathons in elf geselecteerde bioscopen.

offbeat.title: MLS schedule Wednesday, July 22: Will Lewandowski debut? Is Messi available?
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer resumed Wednesday, July 22, with a full 15-match slate after a World Cup break. Inter Miami hosted Chicago Fire in a game that could feature Robert Lewandowski’s MLS debut. Lionel Messi’s availability remained uncertain ahead of the match. The league continues with another 15 games Saturday before the July 29 All-Star Game against Liga MX.

offbeat.title: “È stata la notte più terrificante della mia vita”: Alberto Rossi perde il suo barboncino Ares, ore di angoscia e poi la svolta
offbeat.summary: Italian actor Alberto Rossi's dog Ares, a brown barboncino, was found after being missing for hours in Caprera. Rossi had been searching for the dog since it escaped during a bike ride, prompting a desperate appeal on social media. Ares was eventually located thanks to the efforts of friends and a drone, and Rossi has expressed gratitude to the community for their support. Rossi and Ares are now back home, where they will undergo a check for ticks.

offbeat.title: France pesticide fight : Macron rejects Monique Barbut’s resignation as farm law opens disputed exemptions
offbeat.summary: France’s environment minister Monique Barbut tried to resign July 22, 2026 over a new farm law allowing controversial pesticides, but President Macron rejected her departure. Parliament had just passed the emergency agricultural law the day before, reopening use of acetamiprid and flupyradifurone despite her objections. Barbut called the exemptions a step backward and said she lacked political means to continue. She remains in post as the government stands by the law.

offbeat.title: Stevenage vs West Ham LIVE Score Updates in Friendly Match
offbeat.summary: West Ham United beat Stevenage 3-1 in a closed-doors preseason friendly at Lamex Stadium on July 22, 2025. Over 50,000 season ticket holders watched the match free on West Ham TV after the club waived the £5 streaming fee. Nuno Espírito Santo’s side, fresh from a 1-1 draw with Southend, now turn focus to their 2025-26 campaign. The fixture sold out entirely to season ticket holders.

offbeat.title: French Model Scout Daniel Siad Linked To Epstein Found Dead
offbeat.summary: French authorities opened an investigation after Daniel Siad, a modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, west of Paris on Wednesday. Prosecutors said Siad was under investigation in France for allegedly helping Epstein recruit and traffic young women, charges he denied. His name appears in over 1,000 records tied to Epstein’s network from declassified documents. Siad also faced a rape allegation from a former Swedish model.

offbeat.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
offbeat.summary: NASA will unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Flight Dynamics Research Facility in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. Agency Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will join the ribbon-cutting ceremony. The event is open only to U.S. media citizens or permanent residents, who must RSVP by July 29. The facility marks a key upgrade for NASA’s aeronautics research capabilities.

offbeat.title: In our opinion: Toyota’s Koji Sato’s Japan commonization strategy is sound, difficult — and familiar
offbeat.summary: Toyota CEO Koji Sato is advocating for parts standardization among Japan's automakers to enhance competitiveness against China's cost advantages. This strategy, reminiscent of Sergio Marchionne's earlier attempts, faces challenges due to U.S. antitrust laws and a history of price-fixing among suppliers. If successful, this initiative could reshape collaboration in the industry and strengthen Japan's automotive sector.

offbeat.title: Jasmine Crockett 'finds a racism underbelly in every single place': Martha MacCalum
offbeat.summary: Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett testified Tuesday before a House oversight task force examining diversity, equity and inclusion policies in law schools. She alleged systemic racism persists across institutions, calling it pervasive in every setting. The hearing focused on DEI programs in legal education amid broader debates over their impact. A report on findings is expected within weeks.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Fifteen people were injured, including one seriously, in a tram collision on the Erasmus Bridge in Rotterdam on Wednesday morning. The accident occurred around 11:10 AM when one tram collided with another, causing significant damage and debris on the street. Emergency services responded quickly, treating some victims on-site while others were transported to the hospital. The bridge is currently closed to all traffic as investigations continue into the cause of the crash.

offbeat.title: MATCH PREVIEW | VALENCIA CF – CD ELDENSE
offbeat.summary: Valencia CF face CD Eldense in a pre-season friendly at the Antonio Puchades Stadium on July 25 at 8:00 PM. This marks Valencia’s first match at the venue following training in Girona. Eldense, promoted to LALIGA HYPERMOTION, last met Valencia in the 2024–25 Copa del Rey, where Valencia won. Free tickets for VCF members are available from July 20 to 23.

offbeat.title: Philipsen wins stage 17 and reopens a battle for the green jersey that seemed closed
offbeat.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026, a 174.7 kilometer race between Chambéry and Voiron. The Belgian rider from Alpecin-Premier Tech took the victory, with Mauro Schmid second and Olav Kooij third. Philipsen's win reopens the battle for the green jersey, closing in on leader Mads Pedersen. The general classification remains unchanged, with the favorites now facing a decisive alpine triptych.

offbeat.title: Schumacher zwengelt Verstappen-geruchten opnieuw aan: "Daar zijn gesprekken gevoerd"
offbeat.summary: Red Bull driver Max Verstappen's future remains uncertain as his manager Raymond Vermeulen and team principal Jos Verstappen were spotted at the McLaren hospitality during the Belgian GP weekend. Ralf Schumacher has reignited speculation over Verstappen's future, citing the unlikely coincidence of their presence. Verstappen has yet to comment on his plans, but most experts believe he will remain with Red Bull in 2027. The Dutch driver's future will likely be clarified in the coming months.

offbeat.title: 'It was easy, really': Inside Awa Fam's speedy transition from Spanish champ to Seattle Storm rookie sensation
offbeat.summary: Awa Fam made history on April 13, 2026, becoming the highest-drafted Spanish player ever selected No. 3 overall by the Seattle Storm. Instead of immediately joining the team, she spent a month balancing Valencia Basket’s Liga Feminina playoffs with remote WNBA training via Zoom calls. Fam helped Valencia secure a fourth straight league title before making her WNBA debut on May 24, playing 20 minutes off the bench in her first game. Seattle coach Sonia Raman called her transition a crash course, throwing her straight into the fire.

offbeat.title: Dream School maakt doorstart met nieuwe gezichten
offbeat.summary: Dream School krijgt een doorstart met een nieuwe schoolleiding. Voetbalscheidsrechter Danny Makkelie wordt de rector en Ouassima Tajmout de mentor, vervangend Eric van ’t Zelfde en Lucia Rijker. Het achtste seizoen verschijnt in 2027 op televisie. De omroep hoopt op een frisse start met de nieuwe presentatoren.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: The Committee on Authorisation to Proceed has rejected the request by prosecutors at the Rome Public Prosecutor’s Office to obtain the chat records between Andrea Delmastro and the restaurateur Mauro Caroccia , who is in custody on mafia charges. Meanwhile, MPs from the Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in the Senate in protest, holding up placards reading ‘release the chat logs’. The sitting was suspended. The occupation of the government benches lasted over half an hour. “Shame on you, shame on you,” shouted the Five Star Movement senators. The centre-right responded in unison: “Clowns, clowns”.

offbeat.title: Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow after the indictment
offbeat.summary: Former Spanish President José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero will give his first interview tomorrow following his indictment for influence peddling on May 19. The interview comes after his appearance at the National Court on June 17. This marks his first public response since the legal proceedings began. Observers expect further developments as the case progresses.

offbeat.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
offbeat.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess potential health factors. Authorities called officers after Fakir was found in a confused state and resisted attempts to calm him. The incident has sparked debate over police use of force and legal protections for officers.

offbeat.title: JK Rowling offers support to Women’s Rights Network after Féile cancellation
offbeat.summary: JK Rowling has publicly backed the Women’s Rights Network Northern Ireland after Féile an Phobail cancelled its Respects, Rights and Reality event. The festival organisers said the event’s focus on women’s sex-based rights clashed with its commitment to marginalised groups, including transgender rights. The network called the cancellation shocking and blamed an organised online campaign targeting its members. The group plans to reschedule the discussion.

offbeat.title: Monaco close in on €30m deal for Nantes’ Matthis Abline
offbeat.summary: Monaco are finalising a €30 million deal to sign Nantes striker Matthis Abline. The 23-year-old has agreed a five-year contract, making him Nantes’ record sale after their relegation to Ligue 2. Monaco aim to bolster their attack as Folarin Balogun may leave following his World Cup success. The move is expected to be completed soon.

offbeat.title: | Comunicado Oficial | Información LVII Trofeo Colombino
offbeat.summary: Thousands of fans are expected to attend the LVII Trofeo Colombino match at the Nuevo Colombino stadium in Huelva, Spain on Wednesday. Due to high expected attendance, fans are advised to arrive early and consider alternative parking options as most nearby areas will be closed for Fiestas Colombinas setup. A designated parking area will be available near Gol Norte, accessible from Avenida del Decano. Traffic congestion is anticipated, potentially causing delays at the stadium's entrances.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals a €1.5 billion allocation for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set to take effect in October. This move is part of a broader €8.5 billion package that includes €7 billion in spending increases, bringing total expenditure to €125 billion in 2027. The government aims to keep more of workers' pay through personal taxation changes. The statement's publication by Minister for Finance Simon Harris and Minister for Public Expenditure Jack Chambers marks a key milestone in shaping the country's economic strategy.

offbeat.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani reversed his pledge to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of legal authority. Mamdani now urges US officials to enforce the International Criminal Court’s arrest warrant, though the US is not an ICC member. Netanyahu faces allegations of war crimes, with Trump vowing he would not be detained during a US visit. Mamdani’s team confirmed no legal avenues exist for local enforcement.

offbeat.title: Machtwechsel im Osten?: Alle Daten zur Landtagswahl in Sachsen-Anhalt
offbeat.summary: A major shift in power is expected in the eastern German state of Saxony-Anhalt as voters head to the polls on September 1, with over 1.7 million eligible citizens set to determine the new balance of power in the state parliament. According to recent polls, the Alternative for Germany party could secure up to 41% of the vote, potentially making it the strongest force in the state. This would be a significant change from the current power dynamics. The outcome of the election will be closely watched as it could have implications for the country's political landscape.

offbeat.title: Israel may equip IDF with anti-submarine aircraft as tensions with Turkey grow
offbeat.summary: Israel's defense establishment is considering purchasing the Boeing P-8 Poseidon anti-submarine aircraft, a maritime patrol and multirole plane capable of detecting submarines and carrying various weapons. The aircraft, used by several countries, has an operational radius of 2,200 kilometers and can be refueled in flight. The purchase is reportedly in response to growing tensions with Turkey. The IDF currently operates the "Oron" strategic intelligence and surveillance aircraft, but the P-8 Poseidon would provide enhanced capabilities.

offbeat.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
offbeat.summary: Wolves’ first public pre-season match of the summer takes place at Maidenhead United this evening. The National League South side hosts Cesar Peixoto’s team in Berkshire on Wednesday 22 July, with Wolves TV providing live coverage starting at 7.30pm. Fans can watch the free stream by signing into their Wolves account, though the match won’t be available on the Wolves App. The next pre-season fixtures will be streamed on Wolves TV for £5 each.

offbeat.title: Il sindaco di Bologna soffia sul fuoco
offbeat.summary: A violent protest in Bologna turned chaotic, leaving 64 police officers injured and six police vehicles damaged. The unrest occurred during a rally called by the city's mayor, Matteo Lepore, in response to the death of Abderrahim Fakir, a 43-year-old man who died during a police intervention. The incident marks the second major disturbance in Italy this week, following a 25th anniversary commemoration of the 1997 G8 protests in Genova.

offbeat.title: Assoluti: 1044 a Firenze, Jacobs, Battocletti e...
offbeat.summary: A record 1044 athletes are set to compete in the Italian National Championships in Florence, Italy, from July 24 to 26. The event marks the city's return as host after 22 years, featuring top athletes including Marcell, Nadia, and Fabbri. The championships will award 40 Italian titles, with the first round, the Challenge, taking place on Friday and Saturday. The competition will conclude on Sunday, setting the stage for Italy's European team selection.

offbeat.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
offbeat.summary: Chelsea FC is on the verge of breaching Uefa's financial rules, having spent between £164m and £210m on player transfers since Xabi Alonso became manager. The club faces a £2.6m fine, but can write off £1.7m if they reduce expenditure and increase revenue by next summer. Chelsea's squad value is estimated at £1.3bn, second only to Manchester City in the Premier League. The club's significant debts remain a concern despite recent player sales generating over £120m.

offbeat.title: Dublin Airport is about to boom. Why is Aer Lingus shrinking itself?
offbeat.summary: Aer Lingus is reducing its flights and staff by 7.7 percent, despite Dublin Airport's passenger cap being lifted and charges set to decrease. This decision comes as International Airlines Group, Aer Lingus' parent company, focuses on operating margins across its subsidiaries. IAG's investment strategy allocates funds based on operating margins, with airlines achieving 12 to 15 percent margins receiving additional investment. Aer Lingus' operating margin is expected to be closely monitored in the coming months.

offbeat.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
offbeat.summary: Austrian defender Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. Wöber joined Leeds in 2023 and made 19 appearances in his debut season, including eight league appearances in the 2024/25 title-winning campaign. He scored one goal for the club. Wöber's departure comes after a loan stint with Werder Bremen last season.

offbeat.title: Las redes sociales de À Punt superan el millón y medio de seguidores
offbeat.summary: À Punt’s social media presence has surpassed 1.5 million followers across all platforms, marking a 32% average growth in the last year. The chain’s Facebook page leads with over 269,000 followers, followed by Instagram with 228,000. Digital content production has also surged, with TikTok posts up 45% in early 2026.

offbeat.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
offbeat.summary: Two Russians were sentenced to 11 and eight years in prison in Angola’s capital, Luanda, for terrorism and spying. Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov were convicted of inciting protests, spreading disinformation, and interfering in next year’s presidential election. Authorities linked them to a Russian political network tied to the Wagner group. Their lawyers sought extradition to Russia, but the court’s decision remains unclear.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged in the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African tourist Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors say the investigation, opened in May 2025, led to formal charges after initial custody in 2025. Carter, a mother of four who managed holiday rentals, was found near her car with stab wounds. Authorities continue to review evidence as the case proceeds.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

entertainment.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
entertainment.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, calling them a "signifier of modernity" that disrupts realism. The director made the comment during a Tuesday interview with Gayle King, emphasizing the shoes' potential to break immersion in period productions like his latest film, "The Odyssey." While actors like Matt Damon and Anne Hathaway navigate ancient Greek settings, Nolan insists comfort must not clash with historical authenticity. The rule applies across his productions, ensuring continuity in his meticulous visual storytelling.

entertainment.title: Sophie Thatcher Is Ready to Move Beyond ‘Yellowjackets’ and Step Out of the Darkness
entertainment.summary: Sophie Thatcher, known for her dark roles in Yellowjackets and Heretic, is shifting gears. The 22-year-old actor, who grew up in a devout Mormon household, is stepping into lighter projects. Next up, she stars in the upcoming thriller The Boogeyman, set for a June 2 release.

entertainment.title: FCC Chair Says ABC’s Decision to Not Broadcast Trump Speech Live Will Be Part of Agency’s Review of Station Licenses
entertainment.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says ABC’s decision to skip live coverage of Donald Trump’s July 16 speech on election security will be reviewed as part of the agency’s early license checks for eight ABC-owned stations. Trump, who claimed China accessed millions of voter files to manipulate past elections, criticized ABC and NBC for not airing his remarks live and suggested their licenses should be revoked. Carr, a Trump appointee, indicated broadcasters may have an obligation to carry presidential addresses. The FCC’s review process could take months.

politics.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
politics.summary: Conservative Party chair Kevin Hollinrake has asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the payment was personal and unconditional, while the Conservatives question if it was compensation for running as an MP. HMRC has not yet commented on the inquiry. Farage has previously stated the funds were for personal security and Brexit campaigning.

politics.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
politics.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar resigned to join Andy Burnham’s government, accepting a peerage and a ministerial role at the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. Rivals in Holyrood accused him of hypocrisy, citing past criticism of the House of Lords. The SNP demanded he donate his salary to charity. The move follows Sarwar’s earlier pledge to abolish the Lords.

politics.title: ‘He knows what he’s doing’: Burnham’s off-the-cuff remarks inspire headlines and headaches
politics.summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s new prime minister, faced fresh scrutiny after backtracking on income tax and rent policies within days of taking office. On Wednesday, he confirmed freezing private sector rents remains under review despite earlier signals it would be abandoned. Burnham admitted his off-the-cuff remarks during the Makerfield byelection had been misinterpreted, calling the reaction exaggerated. The government now faces pressure to clarify its economic plans as the opposition demands consistency.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers will appeal a four-match touchline ban given to manager Derek McInnes for calling a late penalty against Celtic "disgusting." The Scottish FA issued the ban after his comments about a stoppage-time spot-kick in Motherwell on May 13, where Celtic won 3-2. McInnes, who was Hearts manager at the time, claimed the decision was incorrect and will contest the penalty. He is set to miss Rangers' opening Scottish Premiership game.

sports.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes faces a four-match touchline ban for calling Celtic’s late penalty against Motherwell "disgusting." The Scottish FA handed the ban over comments made after Hearts’ 13 May loss, where Celtic won 3-2 to stay level on points before beating Hearts to the title days later. McInnes, who was Hearts manager at the time, insists his criticism was aimed at the decision, not referees. He will miss Rangers’ opening Scottish Premiership fixture.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league confirmed it is reviewing the move following a settlement between Miami and the LA Galaxy over Casemiro’s discovery rights. MLS has not disclosed the settlement terms and will not comment until the investigation concludes. The league’s rules grant a club first refusal over a player on its discovery list.

breaking.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
breaking.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. French prosecutors confirmed an investigation into his death and noted Siad’s name appeared thousands of times in released Epstein files. Authorities had previously investigated Siad for alleged human trafficking and conspiracy but found insufficient evidence for arrest. The case remains under review despite his death.

breaking.title: Daniel Siad, Model Scout Who Connected Women to Epstein, Is Found Dead in France
breaking.summary: Daniel Siad, who was found dead on Monday, repeatedly introduced Jeffrey Epstein to young women, according to messages he exchanged with Mr. Epstein. Mr. Siad had denied wrongdoing.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

facts.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
facts.summary: A robotic servicing mission launched from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, carrying NASA-supported technology to service satellites in geosynchronous orbit. The Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV), equipped with NASA’s Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload, will use advanced robotics to inspect and upgrade spacecraft by installing propulsion modules. Funded by DARPA and developed with the U.S. Naval Research Laboratory, the mission aims to extend the lifespan of existing satellites. The MRV represents the first multi-mission robotic in-space servicer, testing new capabilities for future space operations.

crisis.title: Aston Martin secures £550m loan deal
crisis.summary: Aston Martin secured a £550 million loan to strengthen its finances amid ongoing struggles. The luxury carmaker, headquartered in Gaydon, Warwickshire, reported a 50% jump in net losses to £493.2 million last year and plans to cut 600 jobs. The loans aim to support future product plans and improve its balance sheet. Half-year results are due on July 29.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

tech.title: Lawsuit Against Meta Over Social Media Addiction Is Dropped
tech.summary: For Meta, the move was a reprieve after it and YouTube were found guilty in another case in March of negligence and personal injury for their platforms’ addictive features.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

tech.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
tech.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: MPs urge Britain to target ultra-processed foods to cut obesity rates. A health committee report highlights sugar and refined carbs as key drivers, not fat or salt alone. Current policies focus on fat reduction, pushing manufacturers to replace it with sweeteners. The call comes as obesity costs the NHS billions annually, with calls for stricter reformulation targets.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s legionnaires’ disease outbreak, bringing the toll to five fatalities among 82 confirmed cases. Health officials report no new diagnoses in six days, with 56 patients discharged and eight still hospitalized. The outbreak stems from cooling towers on Manhattan’s Upper East Side, where 34 tested positive for legionella bacteria. Further test results are pending as the city continues its investigation.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
